html:
Algorithmic bias occurs when a system produces systematically different outcomes across groups. Sources include biased training data, biased problem framing, and feedback loops that reinforce existing disparities.

There are multiple formal definitions of fairness — and they are mathematically incompatible. You cannot simultaneously satisfy:

  • Demographic parity: equal positive prediction rates across groups
  • Equalized odds: equal true positive and false positive rates across groups
  • Calibration: predicted probabilities match actual outcomes equally across groups

This is not a bug — it is a theorem (Chouldechova, 2017). Choosing a fairness criterion is a value judgement, not a technical one.

Discussion prompt: If you had to choose one fairness criterion for a hiring algorithm, which would you choose and what are you giving up?
